english: his Dhyani Bodhisattwa is associated. In later times there is even mentioned a supreme or highest god, Adi-Buddha, tóg-mai (thog mai) Saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas), which doctrine, however, seems not to have been generally accepted. — Cf. Köpp. II, 15—29.
frost, cold air, cold, coldness, sád-kyis (sad kyis) k'yér-ba (khyer ba) to be destroyed by frost Glr.; often in conjunction with sér-ba (ser ba), hail.

I. to examine, see, try, test, k'yod (khyod) bzód-dam (bzod dam) mi (mi) bzod (bzod) sád-par (sad par) byao (bya'o) I shall see, whether you are patient Dzl.; gser (gser) ltar (ltar) sád-nas (sad nas) mṅón-par (mngon par) gyur (gyur) like gold, it is approved by testing Dzl.; nyáms-sad-pa (nyams sad pa) v. nyams (nyams); sád-mi (sad mi) mi (mi) bdun (bdun) 'the seven men of trial', i.e. the seven most distinguished and talented among the young Tibetans sent by king Kri-sróṅ-lde-btsan (khri srong lde btsan) to K'anpo (mkhan po) Bodhisattwa, for being thoroughly instructed in religion and sciences Glr. 86, also Tar. 162, 22; las (las) sád-pai (sad pa'i) gáṅ-zag (gang zag) Mil. a tried, a tested man? II. frq. in conjunction with gnyid (gnyid), resp. mnal (mnal) 1. to cease to sleep, to awake, rmis (rmis) ma-tág-tu (ma thag tu) directly from that dream Dzl., gzim-pa-las (gzim pa las) from sleep Dzl. — 2. to rouse, from sleep, to waken, more precisely sád-par (sad par) byéd-pa (byed pa); also fig.: dgé-bai (dge ba'i) rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) good, virtuous, emotions Tar.
Pers. سنگین, bayonet W.
col. for sa-heb (sa heb).
Schr., brtsam-grwa (brtsam grwa) Cs., others: bsám-kra (bsam bkra), sáb-dra (sab dra), pocket-book, note-book, memorandum-book, tablets C., W. (Cs. a small writing-desk?).
Ld., yar-sam (yar sam) Lh. mustaches.
with a low voice, lowly, softly, e.g. *zér-cè* (zer ce), from politeness etc.; *sam-súm* (sam sum) *zer* (zer) speak in a low voice! W.

1. termin. of sa (sa), cè-sar (ce sar) dón-pa (don pa) Cs. to promote to high rank or dignity, sar-gnas-dpá-bo (sar gnas dpa' bo) (in a hymn) was explained by: sa (sa) bcú-la (bcu la) gnás-pai (gnas pa'i) dpá-bo (dpa' bo) i.e. Buddha. — 2. sár-gcod-pa (sar gcod pa) v. gsar (gsar). — 3. sbst. wick W.
Ssk. soda, in Wdṅ. it is mentioned as a plant, yielding soda.
v. sa-ra-so-re (sa ra so re).
W., and prob. also sal-sil (sal sil) Mil., gold ornament, gold lace, and the like, ni. f.
clear, bright, brilliant Mil., sal-lér (sal ler) snáṅ-ba (snang ba) lighted up brilliantly, well lighted Pth., mdaṅs (mdangs) sal-sál (sal sal) id. Pth.
instr. of sa (sa).

1. in some parts of Ld. the termination of the instrum. after vowels, like su (su) in Kun. — 2. also si-si (si si), the sound of whistling through the teeth, si-skad (si skad), si-sgra (si sgra) whistling, whistle; si-brda (si brda) whistling, whistle, as a call or sign; si-glu (si glu) a whistled tune. — 3. num.: 58.
n. of one of the four fabulous streams of the world.
1. pack-thread, twine C., W. — 2. bar, bolt, door-bar, *si-ri cúg-cè* (si ri cug ce) or gyáb-'cè* (rgyab ce) to bolt, to bolt up, *si-ri tón-cè* (si ri ton ce) to un-bolt, unbar W.
centiped W.
a kind of inkhorn, case for carrying an inkstand in one's pocket Ld.
Ssk. सिह्लकी a sort of incense.
the breaking up of the ice Sch.
to hitch up, to give a hitch, as porters do with a load on their back Cs.
Sch., síg-ra (sig ra) Schw., a sort of basket.
v. siṅ-siṅ (sing sing).
Sch. curdled milk, sour milk.
Cs., siṅ-ga-lai (sing ga la'i) gliṅ (gling) Glr., सिंहल, Ceylon.
1. frq. for seṅ-ge (seng ge). — 2. v. rtsiṅ-ge (rtsing ge).
